key_insight: Platinum photoelectron spectra are shaped by both many-body lifetime/loss satellites and strong relativistic effects, and the paper builds a unified spectral assignment across shallow/deep core and valence states.
engineering_relevance: This provides a reliable reference for interpreting Pt XPS/HAXPES in catalysts and 5d materials, improving electronic-structure analysis and defect/oxidation-state attribution.
method: RHEELS to map loss features and plasmonic excitations
SXPS and HAXPES to probe shallow and deep core-level line shapes
Ab initio DFT and G0W0 calculations with/without spin-orbit coupling
evidence_signal: The abstract reports consistent agreement between measured spectra, loss features, and calculated orbital-projected densities of states, enabling assignment of satellites, spin-orbit splittings, and valence-band structure.
watch_item: Spectral interpretation depends on careful separation of overlapping loss processes and the inclusion of spin-orbit effects, so simplified fits may misassign satellites or electronic states.
paper_title: Lifetime effects and satellites in the photoelectron spectrum of platinum metal
